qa.overall_assessment: The iteration 3 spec is substantially correct in its 13-step operation order, exit code taxonomy, grant-preservation guarantees, and test isolation protocol. Two acceptance criteria (AC9 and AC10) contain mechanically broken verification recipes that cannot be satisfied simultaneously with the correct implementation text. These are blocking spec_text_vs_execution_drift violations per QA Dimension 5. All other checks pass.

issues.title: AC9 negative grep matches correct Step 4 prose — false positive blocks valid implementation
issues.description: AC9 requires that `grep -E 'os\.unlink|rm -f.*grant|unlink.*grant' commands/push.md` exits with code 1 (no match). However, the spec-mandated Step 4 text already in commands/push.md (line 146) reads: 'The orchestrator MUST NOT read, validate, or unlink the push-analyst grant manually'. This line contains 'unlink' followed by 'grant' and matches the `unlink.*grant` pattern. Running the AC9 negative grep against the correctly updated file produces exit code 0 (match found), causing the AC to FAIL even though Step 4 is correctly worded. The AC9 recipe produces a false positive on the correct prose text.
issues.evidence: Verified via: `grep -E 'os\.unlink|rm -f.*grant|unlink.*grant' /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/push.md` → output: '146:The orchestrator MUST NOT read, validate, or unlink the push-analyst grant manually,' exit code 0. Codex independently confirmed this by running the same grep and getting the same result.
issues.proposed_fix:
Replace the negative grep in AC9 with a code-block-scoped check that only looks for manual unlink instructions inside code fences (``` blocks), not prose text. The corrected verification recipe is:

  awk '/```/{in_code=!in_code} in_code{print}' commands/push.md | grep -E 'os\.unlink\(|rm -f .*grant|unlink .*grant'

This should exit code 1 on the correct Step 4 text. The positive assertion for the 'MUST NOT ... unlink' prose should remain a positive grep (not negative), e.g., `grep -i 'MUST NOT.*unlink' commands/push.md` returning at least one match is the correct positive assertion.

issues.title: AC10 plain grep cannot verify --branch and other args on multiline invocation continuation lines
issues.description:
AC10 asserts that `grep 'execute-push.py' commands/push.md` output 'includes the literal string python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py (tilde form) with --branch AND --request-id AND --repo-hash AND --remote arguments visible'. The spec-mandated Step 5 invocation is a multiline shell block with argument continuation lines:

  python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py \
    --repo-hash "${REPO_HASH}" \
    --branch "${BRANCH}" \
    --remote "${RESOLVED_REMOTE}" \
    --request-id "${REQUEST_ID}"

A plain `grep 'execute-push.py'` will return only the line containing 'execute-push.py' — this line ends with a backslash continuation and does NOT contain --branch, --request-id, --repo-hash, or --remote. Those arguments appear on the following continuation lines which grep does not include. The AC10 assertion that the grep output contains all four argument names is mechanically impossible for a multiline invocation matching the spec's required format.
issues.evidence: Verified by reading commands/push.md Step 5 (lines 182-193) which shows the current invocation format. The spec's desired replacement (ticket-20260524-125300-push.md lines 213-227) uses the same multiline backslash-continuation format. Codex independently flagged this issue and confirmed that plain grep on the script name line will not show continuation args.
issues.proposed_fix:
Replace the AC10 grep recipe with one that extracts the block including continuation lines, then asserts all required args are present. The corrected verification recipe is:

  grep -A8 -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md

Then assert that the captured block (8 lines after the matching line) contains '--repo-hash', '--branch', '--remote', and '--request-id'. The AC check object should be updated to:

  {
    "invocation_uses_tilde_form": "grep -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md returns match",
    "invocation_contains_all_args": "grep -A8 -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md contains --repo-hash AND --branch AND --remote AND --request-id"
  }

passed_checks.title: 13-step operation order is internally consistent
passed_checks.details: All 13 steps are non-contradictory. Steps proceed in correct dependency order: args parse → SESSION_ID read → grant path compute → push.sh check → read grant → parse grant → validate fields → act on verdict → check HEAD drift → consume grant → write sentinel → set env → execv. Each step's abort condition is consistent with its position (steps 1-4 are infrastructure, steps 5-9 are validation, steps 10-13 are the commit+exec sequence).

passed_checks.title: Exit code taxonomy is consistent across all spec sections
passed_checks.details: Exit 2 for infrastructure failures (SESSION_ID unset, push.sh missing, execv OSError) is consistent across the interface spec table, MoSCoW items M3/M4/M11, operation order steps 1/2/4/13, and ACs 6/7/8. Exit 1 for validation failures (grant absent, parse fail, field mismatch, blocked verdict, HEAD drift, grant-consume failure) is consistent across M4/M5/M6/M7/M8, operation order steps 5-10, and ACs 2/3/4. No contradictions found.

passed_checks.title: grant.head_sha is the ONLY HEAD drift check — no --pre-head anywhere
passed_checks.details: Searched ticket-20260524-125300-push.md and acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-push.json for '--pre-head', 'pre_head', 'PRE_HEAD' — no matches found. The single HEAD check at operation step 9 uses git rev-parse HEAD compared to grant.head_sha, consistent across M7, the operation order, and AC3.

passed_checks.title: Grant preserved on all steps 5-9 failures
passed_checks.details: Each failure path explicitly preserves the grant: step 5 (file not found) — grant never touched; step 6 (parse fail) — grant never touched; step 7 (field mismatch) — 'grant NOT consumed' stated explicitly; step 8 (blocked verdict) — 'grant NOT consumed' stated explicitly; step 9 (HEAD drift) — 'grant NOT consumed' stated explicitly. Grant consume is deferred to step 10 which is only reached after steps 5-9 all pass. Consistent across M5/M6/M7/M8 and ACs 2/3/4.

passed_checks.title: Test isolation is adequate
passed_checks.details: HOME=<tmpdir> isolation correctly separates mock push.sh from the live symlink at ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh. The script is invoked by absolute path (/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py), so HOME redirection only affects the push.sh lookup path. MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH protocol is consistent throughout. The 'NEVER write to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh' constraint is clearly stated in the test isolation rule. AC7's mechanism (non-existent shebang interpreter → OSError from execve) was confirmed reliable on Linux by Codex's live probe (returncode=2, FileNotFoundError).

passed_checks.title: Sentinel schema matches push.sh consumer exactly
passed_checks.details: Spec M9 mandates exactly 5 keys: result='PASS', request_id, head (raw branch name), branch (raw branch), remote — all non-empty strings. push.sh lines 137-150 verify exactly these 5 fields with the same names and the same 'non-empty string AND equal to expected value' semantics. The branch encoding (branch.replace('/', '__') for path, raw branch name in JSON field) matches push.sh lines 94-98 and 118 (_CHAIN_B_BRANCH_RAW as the expected value).

passed_checks.title: AC6 grant preservation on push.sh absent is consistent with step 4
passed_checks.details: Operation order step 4 says push.sh check happens 'before any grant file is touched'. M4 says 'exit 2 if not, before any grant file is touched'. AC6 checks grant_still_exists=true and sentinel_absent=true. All three are consistent — push.sh check at step 4 is earlier than grant read at step 5, so a step-4 failure preserves the grant (it hasn't been read, let alone consumed).

passed_checks.title: commands/push.md scope is correctly bounded
passed_checks.details: Spec correctly identifies Steps 4+5 as the update scope. Step 4 is already updated (MUST NOT language present). Step 5 still has /root/.claude/ absolute form and missing --branch (confirmed by reading commands/push.md lines 182-186). The spec's required Step 5 replacement text (ticket lines 213-227) is well-defined. AC9/AC10 are the correct verification targets for these changes (though their grep recipes are broken as documented in ISSUE-1 and ISSUE-2).

passed_checks.title: AC7 execv OSError mechanism is reliable on Linux
passed_checks.details: Confirmed by Codex live probe: creating a script with shebang #!/path/to/missing-interpreter and calling os.execv on it returns FileNotFoundError (errno=2), exit code 2. This matches the AC7 expected behavior (exit 2, M11 sentinel cleanup path triggers). The mechanism is reliable via POSIX execve semantics: kernel returns ENOENT when shebang interpreter is missing.

passed_checks.title: AC5 subprocess.run stderr capture valid across os.execv
passed_checks.details: Confirmed by Codex live probe: fd 2 (stderr) survives os.execv as an open file descriptor. subprocess.run(stderr=subprocess.PIPE) creates a pipe connected to the subprocess's fd 2; when os.execv replaces the Python process image, the execv'd process (mock push.sh) inherits fd 2 still connected to the pipe. Since mock push.sh is specified to NOT write to stderr, only execute-push.py's warn-path output appears in the captured bytes. The probe confirmed stdout= 'risk-one' in captured stderr with returncode=0.

passed_checks.title: S1 vs M4 redundancy is not a blocking defect
passed_checks.details: S1 (Should Have) describes the same push.sh existence check that M4 (Must Have) already mandates. Both require exit 2 at step 4. This creates S1 as a redundant Should Have when the behavior is already required. Not blocking — it cannot cause a contradiction; it only creates documentation confusion. BA may optionally remove S1 or reword it to add diagnostic message specificity.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed draft verdict should be downgraded from PASS to 'APPROVED after spec patch' due to two mechanically broken AC verification recipes (ISSUE-1: AC9 negative grep catches correct prose text; ISSUE-2: AC10 plain grep cannot see multiline invocation args). Codex also confirmed: AC7 non-existent shebang mechanism is reliable on Linux (confirmed via live probe), AC5 stderr pipe survives execv (confirmed via live probe), S1/M4 redundancy is not blocking, and current commands/push.md Step 5 state is an existing doc gap not a BA spec defect. Codex raised 6 findings total; Issues 3-6 adjudicated as out_of_scope or minor observations per scope rules.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: ISSUE-1 and ISSUE-2 from Codex are promoted to blocking findings in this QA report, resulting in verdict FAIL. Issues 3 (branch drift), 4 (execv vs subprocess proof), 5 (--auto forwarding coverage), 6 (R7 traceability) are recorded as out_of_scope_observations per scope rules — they are coverage improvements, not spec contradictions.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex Issue 3: spec validates grant.branch == --branch but does not require verifying current_branch == --branch. If user switches to another branch pointing at same HEAD between grant creation and execv, sentinel is written with wrong branch (though push.sh will catch it downstream via its own branch binding check). This is a security hardening suggestion beyond this spec's scope.

Codex Issue 4: no AC proves os.execv (same-PID) vs subprocess.run (child PID). The core security guarantee is same-PID replacement; the current ACs verify exec'd behavior but not the PID identity. Codex suggests extending mock to record $$ and asserting Popen.pid == captured PID.

Codex Issue 5: --auto forwarding (M10 Must Have) has no AC that specifically captures $2/$@ in the mock invocation. The mock capture protocol writes only $1 (remote) to MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH. A separate AC or an extension to AC1 should verify that --auto is present in the execv argv when the flag is passed.

Codex Issue 6 / QA-observed: R7 in requirements_decomposition does not mention --branch, --repo-hash, --remote as required args, while AC10 checks for all four. Minor traceability gap — AC is more specific than the requirement. BA should update R7 acceptance criterion to name all four args.

ba_validation_dimensions.evidence_quality: pass — all factual claims in the spec are backed by source reads (hooks/push.sh lines cited, agents/push-analyst.md lines cited, grep patterns verified in session). Codex cross-validated by reading the actual files.
ba_validation_dimensions.scope_alignment: pass — scope correctly covers scripts/execute-push.py (create) and commands/push.md (Steps 4+5). Out-of-scope items (hooks/push.sh internals, agents/push-analyst.md, settings.json) are correctly excluded.
ba_validation_dimensions.investigation_completeness: pass — BA traced the sentinel schema from push.sh:118-152, traced the grant schema from agents/push-analyst.md:148-182, confirmed execute-push.py absence, and identified both the timing-window root cause and the subagent-auth-boundary failure mode.
ba_validation_dimensions.affected_file_accuracy: pass — scripts/execute-push.py (to create) and commands/push.md (to modify Steps 4+5) are the correct affected files. hooks/push.sh is correctly identified as read-only infra.
ba_validation_dimensions.spec_text_vs_execution_drift:
FAIL — two AC verification recipes cannot execute the literal checks they claim:
1. AC9: negative grep 'unlink.*grant' catches correct Step 4 prose text, causing false positive (exit 0 when exit 1 expected)
2. AC10: plain grep 'execute-push.py' on multiline backslash-continuation invocation shows only the script-name line, not the arg lines; assertion that output contains --branch etc. is mechanically impossible for the spec-required multiline format

qa.approval_notes: REJECTED — iteration 3 resolves all 5 NEW-B and 5 NEW-M blockers from iteration 2, and the 13-step operation order, exit code taxonomy, grant preservation, and test isolation are all correct. However, two AC verification recipes are mechanically broken in ways that cannot be resolved by the dev implementation: AC9's negative grep fires on correct Step 4 prose, and AC10's plain grep cannot verify multiline invocation arguments. Both are spec_text_vs_execution_drift violations requiring BA to update the AC literal text before dev work proceeds. Required changes are precise and minimal (documented in ISSUE-1 and ISSUE-2 proposed fixes). Re-submit as iteration 4 with only these two AC recipe corrections applied.
